item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An enjoably surreal image and as Kalinin said, some fun dream logic. For whatever reason, though, it didn't really grab me. The only thing I know about the governor is what is brought up in the article, and it's not enough to form a compelling story for me. Novote

item.title: Re: Development notes
item.content:encoded: I'm very happy record of this exists.

item.title: Development notes
item.content:encoded:
Found the log of our development chat!

+ September 23, 2015
- unless you're not interested

<Roget> greetings
<Voct> okay, so your current project is Napoleon Broward's corpse, fused to an Eco-Wrecker?
<Voct> is that it?
<Roget> I'm going for Broward being in purgatory, but yeah thats the object

  • Voct nods

<Voct> okay, here are some points
<Voct> I'm pretty sure molasses is a lot *less* viscous than tar
<Voct> use wet cement.
<Roget> I shall
<Voct> why is this keter. Why can't it be Euclid.
<Voct> just keep dumping wet cement on it
<Voct> next
<Voct> Any attempts to exhume the grave of Napoleon Bonaparte Broward are to be blocked by Foundation agents in the Florida judicial
system
<Voct> here's an interesting point
<Voct> (leaving aside the question of whether a grave can be exhumed, or just a body)
<Voct> why would they block this?
<Roget> cos then they'd see the grave is empty
<Roget> and that would be bad for containment
<Voct> why would someone try to exhume Broward?
<Voct> and here's an interesting point
<Voct> what iffffff
<Voct> what if the Foundation exhumed Broward already
<Voct> found that he's still in the grave
<Voct> there's two Browards
<Voct> one who's trapped in the tar pit
<Voct> one who's rotting in the grave
<Roget> Hm
<Roget> Interesting
<Voct> also
<Voct> Broward is in hell, yes?
<Voct> not just purgatory
<Voct> but actual hell
<Roget> yeah
<Roget> he definitely went to hell
<Voct> what iffffff
<Voct> what if the dredger is _fixing_ the environment
<Voct> it's undoing all of Broward's hard work
<Voct> the machine is making the swamp prettier at a rate of 100 units / day
<Voct> while Broward's zombie is frantically trying to make the swamp more civilized at a rate of maybe 2 units per day
<Roget> So the dredger is fixing things up while he pukes lead and flails impotently
<Voct> yes
<Roget> nice
<Voct> ….. also, the Foundation has been dumping heavily contaminated waste on the dredger
<Voct> in hopes that it will fix it.
<Roget> fix it?
<Voct> minute
<Voct> i looked at something bright and now i've got phosphenes
<Voct> they make it hard to read the screen
<Voct> please hold, someone will be with you shortly
<Roget> kk
<Voct> here's an interesting point
<Voct> why has his hell been outsourced to earth?
<Roget> Broward died before they invented space
<Voct> space?
<Voct> the point is
<Voct> Giraffe Hell is an SCP in a subdimension
<Voct> Ice Cream Hell is an SCP in a subdimension
<Voct> why has Napoleon Broward's hell been set on Earth instead of a replicated Earth environment?
<Voct> and how much longer before these damn phosphenes finally fade
<Roget> 2.5 kilometers
<Voct> please, I'm discussing an actual health condition right now. Don't joke about it.
<Roget> Sure, apologies
<Voct> why has Napoleon Broward's hell been set on Earth instead of a replicated Earth environment?
<Roget> I think because he spent so much of his life tearing up the earth, that he'd stay to reap what he sowed
<Voct> why not Thomas Midgley, then
<Roget> because I don't live in a county named after him
<Voct> Thomas Midgley invented leaded gasoline *and* the use of CFCs as a refrigerant
<Roget> I'm aware
<Roget> he's probably in a deeper hell
<Voct> there has to be a reason
<Voct> I'm thinking
<Voct> the environmental remediation that the dredger does
<Voct> isn't permanent
<Voct> and
<Voct> hm
<Voct> on the occasions when Dredger falls asleep, exhausted
<Voct> the Dredger has spoken to the Foundation
<Voct> apologizing for having to bring him up here for his punishment
<Voct> they've been having budget problems
<Voct> "budget problems?" "Oops, he's waking up! Shh!"
<Roget> hahahahaha
<Roget> lmao
<Roget> perfect
<Voct> that's also why the remediation isn't permanent
<Voct> it just gets fixed long enough to make Broward miserable again
<Roget> So, dredger is fixing Broward's bile, and he's trying so hard and exhausing himself so the dredger is apologizing for hell having to
bring him up to make room?
<Voct> yeah
<Voct> They don't currently have the budget to create a specific subplane of hell just for him
<Voct> DNA samples from Broward-merged-with-Dredger and Broward-in-grave match
<Voct> as do dental records
<Voct> and damage inflicted on Broward-in-grave has appeared on Broward-merged-with-Dredger
<Voct> like, if you use a laser to burn a slice in the inaminate Broward's forehead….
<Voct> that slice will appear in the animate Broward's forehead
<Voct> (but not vice versa)
<Voct> you're familiar with Sisyphyus?
<Roget> yesh
<Voct> Zombie Broward has told the Foundation that as soon as he has drained and paved all the swamps in America, he will be able to
rest
<Voct> the Dredger works almost exactly the opposite of how he wants it to
<Voct> ah, and the phosphenes have faded
<Voct> you know what I mean by 'phosphene', yes?
<Roget> I looked it up after you told me not to joke aboot it
<Roget> hows it look now?
<Voct> sorry, I was dealing with garbage
<Voct> literally, things I needed to put in the recycling bin and/or garbage cans
<Voct> checking
<Roget> kk
<Voct> why technetium?
<Voct> especially since it's not something naturally found?
<Roget> its crazy poisonous
<Voct> I don't think Hell has the budget to produce technetium
<Voct> at the hip with a dredging device (SCP-2489-2) used during his tenure as governor to drain the Everglades wetlands.
<Voct> are you saying that the Foundation tracked down each and every dredging device that was used during Broward's term, found
trhat there was one unaccounted for, and then concluded that it must be the animate one?
<Roget> When it activated it was one of the ones that's sitting out in the everglades right now
<Roget> Did you know they actually just left them out there, and those dredges are still out there to this day?
<Roget> I've been to one
<Roget> it's pretty cool
<Voct> !
<Voct> SCP-2489-1 is the re-animated corpse of Napoleon Bonaparte Broward1
<Voct> no, no
<Voct> SCP-2489-1 is *apparently* the animated corpse of Napoleon Bonaparte Broward
<Voct> SCP-2489-1 is an animated cadaver identifying itself as Napoleon Bonaparte Broward
<Voct> hello?
<Voct> skip the technetium. Just use lead and mercury.
<Roget> hi hi
<Roget> I thought I'd responded in the affirmitive, sorry
<Roget> Anything else to say about its current state?
<Voct> hm
<Voct> any and all land which could be used for human habitat
<Voct> any and all *uncultivated* land, with the apparent goal of making it fit for human habitation
<Voct> 'BRIM BELOW OUT OF SPACE WILL REMEDY AT A POINT
<Voct> how about
<Voct> 'APOLOGIES BRIM BELOW OUT OF SPACE WILL REMEDY AT A POINT'
<Roget> do you think an interview with Broward would be good?
<Voct> she hmm
<Voct> sure
<Voct> he'll definitely ask them if they're voters
<Voct> oh, and
<Voct> how would he react to being approached by someone black?
<Roget> probably very dismissive to them
<Voct> he'd ask to speak to a white person
<Voct> he has no idea what year it is
<Roget> I think the Foundation would probably plan ahead and send a white person
<Voct> even if you tell him what year it is, he'll completely misunderstand you
<Voct> 2015? You mean 1915, obviously.
<Roget> He'd probably like talking about water
<Roget> since he was a river boat cap'm
<Roget> and then went on to drain the glades
<Roget> shit was his life
<Roget> thanks for all the help on this btw
<Voct> why is he keter
<Voct> at worst he's euclid
<Voct> also, you should specify that the dredger he's fused with is an actual historic dredger
<Voct> and that it doesn't appear to require fuel
<Roget> kk
<Voct> please explain why it's keter
<Roget> It's constantly moving to breach containment
<Roget> I'm heading to bed now, but this has been very helpful, I may come to you again once I've developed it a bit more

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Subtle and it connects it more to the glades, I heartily approve

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright, so this thing is hard to contain, and that's keeping it stuck in a tar pit where they found it in the Everglades. If it got out, making sure that it didn't get anywhere near SCP-2147 would likely be a high priority, cause you don't want it jumping ship and running a muck in Ohio, where there are significantly more people. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

In the event that SCP-2489 breaches containment, Foundation agents are to move to pre-designated locations in order to head off any potential appearances by SCP-2489. Agents embedded in major media sources are to suppress any reports of SCP-2489 activity, and a Foundation front company 'Samson Chemical and Petroleum' blamed for any ecological damage.

To this:

In the event that SCP-2489 breaches containment, Foundation agents are to move to pre-designated locations in order to head off any potential appearances by SCP-2489. Agents embedded in major media sources are to suppress any reports of SCP-2489 activity, and a Foundation front company 'Samson Chemical and Petroleum' blamed for any ecological damage. At no point should SCP-2489 be allowed to access SCP-2147-A.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wierd and unnerving in just the right way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article is one that, as said by previous members who commented, an article that is definitely strange, yet good. It held my attention with a firm grip and kept me hooked. Great work, Roget.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very strange. I can tell you spent a lot of time on this. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Feels a lot like a Series I article. I like it.

Also thanks for not ruining it by explaining its origins in excruciating detail in an addendum/interview/recovered document that's longer than the article itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I started drafting this one last summer so it's been in the works for awhile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did this one get posted and deleted once, or did I just see it a super-long time ago? Because I remember it!

I would codify "amplified voice" (give a dB rating or something), but otherwise, this is great. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've got no bloody idea what I just read. But its weird. Waaaay to much weird. And for that I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was a very entertaining piece!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, weird, and entertaining. Especially the line about 2489-1 being in favor of forest fires.

+1. This was a fun read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very weird and just the right amount of unsettling. In my personal headcanon I imagine it as some horrific karmic afterlife as punishment for messing with the environment. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bizarre but not ridiculous, and elegant in its conciseness. The skip deserves my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been waiting for more stuff like this. It strikes a consistent, weird internal logic and narrative tone, without requiring seventeen addenda and a Note at the End. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image credit here: http://sofia.usgs.gov/sfrsf/rooms/historical/future/mvdredgenx.jpg

Thanks to Voct, Quik, rume and others for helping with this one.
